titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Samuel Clark Harvey Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by John Espy and the Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 08:47

did.abstract: Personal and professional papers of Samuel Clark Harvey.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale University Historical Medical Library, 1980. Gift of Louise Guion and Elizabeth Harvey, 2003.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Samuel Clark Harvey Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in five series and one addition: I. Correspondence and Lectures. II. Research Materials. III. Writings. IV. Writings of Others and Miscellaneous. V. Papers Re: Books and Bibliography.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Samuel C. Harvey (1886-1953): surgeon; Ph.B., Yale, 1907, M.D., 1911; instructor of surgery, Yale University, 1919-1921, associate professor, 1921-1924, William H. Carmalt professor of surgery, 1924-1947, professor of surgery (oncology), 1947-1952; retired and continued research in field.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal and professional papers of Samuel Clark Harvey.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Katharine Kingsley (Farnam) Harvey (Mrs. S.C. Harvey): ALS to F. G. Kilgour re: S. C. Harvey's papers
Personal correspondence, with miscellaneous notes, typescripts, printed matter, mostly re: history of medicine
Correspondence and printed matter from medical associations
Hawaii Territorial Medical Association: 58th Annual Meeting, 1948 May: correspondence, typescripts of lectures, audio records, printed matter
"The Breaking Strength of Healing Fractured Fibulae of Rats," study by S. C. Harvey, R. M. McKeown, and others: notes, typescripts, x-rays, charts, printed matter
Burn wound treatment, and general wound healing: correspondence, notes, MSS, typescripts, patient records, printed matter (including bulletins of the National Research Council, Subcommittee on Burns)
Burn wound treatment, and general wound healing: correspondence, notes, MSS, typescripts, patient records, printed matter (including bulletins of the National Research Council, Subcommittee on Burns)
Chlorophyll: correspondence, notes, typescripts, printed matter
Contraceptives and cancer: correspondence, printed matter
History of Surgery: correspondence, notes, typescripts, printed matter

[History of Surgery?]: miscellaneous materials, including Harold S. Arnold's biographical materials on Bishop George Berkeley and Dr. William H. Carmalt; notes on influenza; and papers collected by S. C. Harvey, including printed by-laws of the Connecticut Medical Society, and one ALS each by Benjamin Silliman and Benjamin Silliman, Jr.
Hospital care and insurance plans: correspondence, printed matter
Microscope slides, apparently re: skin of dogs and humans
Bibliography on war medicine and surgery
John Cecil Holm, "Golden Harvest": mimeographs of play
Miscellaneous printed matter by others, with S. C. Harvey's notes and correspondence
Printed matter (especially obituaries) about S. C. Harvey and S. C. Harvey Memorial Scholarship, with photograph
Departmental correspondence, and papers re: Historical Medical Library
Scrapbooks of publishers' advertisements re: historical medical books
Bibliographical note cards, mostly re: history of medicine; possibly an inventory of Historical Medical Library
Harvey, Katharine Farman [includes family documents, memorabilia, printed matter]
Yale Medical School and related publications
Scrapbook [World War I, American Expeditionary Forces, the Harvard unit organized by Harvey Cushing, M.D.]
